today_news.title: Google's new open model DiffusionGemma generates text from noise instead of word by word
today_news.summary: Google released DiffusionGemma, a 26-billion-parameter model that generates text not token by token but through diffusion, similar to how image AI turns noise into a picture. According to Nvidia, it hits about 1,000 tokens per second on a single H100 GPU, roughly four times faster than comparable au...

today_news.title: OpenAI's IPO slips as Altman tells staff to expect a public offering "within the next year"
today_news.summary: Sam Altman told employees he expects an OpenAI IPO "within the next year," but a delay to 2027 is possible. He frames it as caution around self-improving AI, though Anthropic's stronger growth numbers and imminent IPO may be the real reason to wait.

today_news.title: OpenAI wants its biggest data center yet, and Nvidia would back the bill
today_news.summary: OpenAI is negotiating to lease a planned 10-gigawatt data center in Ohio that could be financially backed by Nvidia, according to The Information.

today_news.title: The Download: the “steroid olympics” and a safer Mythos
today_news.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. today_news.title: Claude Fable 5: Anthropic admits "wrong tradeoff" after invisibly throttling rival AI researchers
today_news.summary: Anthropic reverses course on a policy that would have secretly undermined AI researchers, but another point of contention persists.

today_news.title: Anthropic study shows AI needs hours, not weeks, to build exploits from security patches
today_news.summary: Anthropic's security team found that its Mythos Preview AI model can turn security patches for Firefox and the Windows kernel into working exploits within hours, for a few thousand dollars and no specialized knowledge. Eight complete attack chains were finished before Microsoft's auto-updates had re...

today_tech.summary: Abstract: Learning-based speech compression has achieved promising low-bitrate performance, but many neural speech codecs still describe quantized latents with preset-rate discrete symbols or apply entropy coding only after symbol generation. today_tech.title: Can Voice Agents Handle Bilingual Customers? Benchmarking Frontier ASR on Code-Switched Speech
today_tech.summary: Over half of the world's population speaks more than one language.
